title: itertools.groupby: a shared groupby iterator advances gbo->currkey/currvalue/currgrouper non-atomically (groupby_next / _grouper_create, Modules/itertoolsmodule.c)
description: itertools.groupby keeps its cross-call state -- currkey, currvalue, tgtkey, and the current child grouper (currgrouper) -- in plain fields on the groupbyobject with no per-object synchronization. When a single groupby iterator is shared across threads (e.g. list(gb) driven from several workers), groupby_next (Modules/itertoolsmodule.c:~537) mutates that shared state (and _grouper_create allocates/links the child grouper) while another thread's groupby_next / _grouper_next reads or writes it -> data race on the groupby cursor. This is the shared stateful-itertools-object class (like count() / TSAN-0006), not the sequence-iterator-cursor class.
sites: Modules/itertoolsmodule.c groupby_next:~537 (WRITE gbo->currkey / currvalue / tgtkey / currgrouper while advancing the shared groupby)
Modules/itertoolsmodule.c _grouper_create (allocate + link the child grouper to the shared groupby) and _grouper_next (the child grouper reads gbo->currkey/currvalue) -- the racing accessors
race_kind: shared stateful-iterator object: non-atomic read/write of the groupby object's cross-call fields (currkey/currvalue/tgtkey/currgrouper) across threads, with no per-object critical section. Same class as itertools.count (TSAN-0006); distinct from the sequence-iterator cursor family.
shared_state: groupbyobject fields currkey, currvalue, tgtkey, currgrouper (and the parent<->child grouper linkage) on a single shared groupby
severity: moderate (a data race on the group cursor -> mis-grouped / duplicated / lost groups, or refcount trouble on currkey/currvalue exchanged without locking -> possible crash under concurrency). Sharing one groupby across threads is unusual (lower real-world priority), but itertools declares no per-object locking here.
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads each doing list(gb) on ONE shared itertools.groupby(range(8192)), x500 rounds) exits 66 on the debug-ft-nojit-tsan build; TSan reports _grouper_create racing groupby_next. Fleet-10 drove the groupby_next self-race face (1 veh).
suggested_fix: Take the groupby object's per-object critical section (Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(gbo)) over groupby_next's read-modify-write of currkey/currvalue/tgtkey/currgrouper (and coordinate _grouper_next's reads of the parent), as the FT hardening did for other stateful itertools iterators. The parent<->child grouper handoff needs to be atomic w.r.t. concurrent advances.
confirmed_build: debug-ft-nojit-tsan (CPython 3.16.0a0, --disable-gil --with-thread-sanitizer); also reproduced on release-ft-nojit-tsan (release + TSan) -> NOT a debug-only artifact
prior_art: ALREADY REPORTED = gh-150791 ('groupby_next data race on free-threaded builds', OPEN), fix in OPEN PR #150792 ('gh-150791: add critical section for groupby.next'). #150791 describes EXACTLY this race: two threads in groupby_next race on gbo->currgrouper -- one writes NULL at :537 while the other stores the new grouper via _grouper_create (:633) -- corrupting state and producing AttributeError on slot accesses of live objects. groupby_next has NO critical section on current main. The earlier gh-143543/#146613 fixes (MERGED) are RE-ENTRANCY UAF fixes (Py_INCREF snapshots around PyObject_RichCompareBool for a user __eq__ re-entering next()) -- ORTHOGONAL to the thread race, no locking added. Found via gh api search (web search missed #150791).
related: Shared stateful-itertools-object class, same as itertools.count TSAN-0006 (#153908). The op-h/shared-object path (P3.1) shares the groupby. Distinct from the sequence-iterator cursor family (TSAN-0037/0038/0039/0040/0026). Iterator-FT strategy = gh-124397 (rhettinger). This one CROSSES the 'don't crash' bar (#150791: corrupts state -> AttributeError on live objects), so it is a genuine fix target -- gh-123471 (the itertools-FT umbrella) does not list groupby, but #150791/#150792 tracks it directly.
notes: NOT a new find: this is gh-150791, fix pending in OPEN PR #150792 (adds Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION to groupby.next). Independently reproduced by fusil (fleet-10, 1 veh) on debug AND release TSan builds (exit 66); the isolated repro (8 threads x list(shared_groupby)) is a simpler trigger than #150791's __eq__-key script. No new filing; a confirmation on #150791 is the only outward-facing step, at the maintainer's discretion. Records the groupby_next self-race + the _grouper_create/_grouper_next child-grouper faces. Re-run repro.py -> status fixed when #150792 merges.
